THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee advanced a nuisance ordinance update and pushed for a county traffic study, while several area boards held or prepared to hold meetings on elections, school standards, and fire department staffing.

  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee unanimously forwarded revised Chapter 382 Nuisances ordinance language to the Village Board for approval at its July 6 meeting.
  • The CLIPP Committee reached consensus to ask Marathon County to conduct a traffic study at County Road XX and Terabonding Drive, with the existing four-way stop staying in place.
  • Kronenwetter staff are set to research costs and grant options for a bike and pedestrian path along Highway X, with a report expected back to the committee in August.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board is set to consider ballot drop box installation and revisions to Chapter 41 Elections, as well as new oak wilt regulations under Chapter 389.
  • The Wausau School Board is set to act on adoption of Wisconsin Academic Standards, WIAA membership renewals for Wausau East, Wausau West, and WAVE, and an EFIS wall repair at South Mountain Elementary.
  • Kronenwetter's Police and Fire Commission is set to interview Fire Department applicants in closed session and consider action on department charge forms and procedures.

Coming up: Wausau's Common Council meets Tuesday at 6:30 p.m., preceded by Finance Committee at 5:15 p.m. and Board of Public Works at 10:30 a.m.; Marathon County's Public Safety Committee also convenes Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
